source-discipline.reason: The DR's premise is a DB-measured facts ledger: search_coverage 4,960 rows (4,286 NOT-RUN), search_languages 1,558 rows (859 NOT-RUN), lang_jur_map 0 rows, search_executions absent — all read-only-queried this session, none asserted. The table shape mirrors established STRICT + json_valid peers (item_population_links, spec_value_probes, evidence_cell_state) rather than inventing a pattern.

canonical-workplan.reason: The DDL implements search-coverage-completion-workplan.md §2.2 verbatim and the coverage-completion-loop methodology §4 (both ratified), not a new plan; it is the substrate those documents specify but never built.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: Commit carries [DOCTRINE: 0f2f525] before the timestamp per rule #11(a); this attestation is co-committed per rule #11(b). doctrine_sha matches HEAD:governance/mission-and-epistemics.md.

bias_direction: Risk of overbuild — a 24-column log authored before a single loop batch has run, the repo's known scaffolding-for-scaffolding failure mode. Mitigated by (a) the thin-logger scope deferring FTS5 and the priority-queue view; (b) every column mapping to one of the five mission-committed coverage axes plus provenance, none speculative; (c) DG-REVIEW gating so the schema is applied only on owner approval, and (d) shipping the table empty so it earns its keep only when the loop writes to it.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could argue the substrate is premature — no cell has been worked, so a full event-log schema precedes any demonstrated need and duplicates search_coverage/search_languages. Counter: the two grids are exactly what is being escaped (they cannot record what was searched, in what language, how deep — the mission's own transparency commitment), coverage is DERIVED from this log so it cannot be faked, and the migration is reversible by a forward 034_drop if a pilot judges it unnecessary. The DR does not apply it; the owner decides.
